description: Full-screen TUI session monitor (`yolomode watch`) — three-panel layout with session picker (left), agent log viewer (center), and diff panel (right). Built with @opentui/core + @opentui/solid (Solid.js), adapting opendocker's container streaming and critique's diff renderer. Red-green TDD with bun test is mandated for all non-UI, non-framework logic: write failing tests first, then implement to pass. Full spec: tasks/prd-watch.md. Epic specs: tasks/prd-watch-epic-1-foundation.md through tasks/prd-watch-epic-5-help-bar.md.

acceptanceCriteria: Run `bun add @opentui/core @opentui/solid solid-js` — all appear in package.json dependencies
Run `bun add @opentui-ui/dialog @opentui-ui/toast` — all appear in package.json dependencies
`import { render } from "@opentui/solid"` resolves without TypeScript error in a .tsx file
tsconfig.json updated with `"jsx": "preserve"` and `"jsxImportSource": "solid-js"`
Typecheck passes
userStories.description: Install @opentui/core, @opentui/solid, solid-js, @opentui-ui/dialog, and @opentui-ui/toast as production dependencies and configure tsconfig.json for Solid.js JSX. Full spec: tasks/prd-watch-epic-1-foundation.md US-001.
userStories.notes: Installed @opentui/core@0.1.80, @opentui/solid@0.1.80, solid-js@1.9.11, @opentui-ui/dialog@0.1.2, @opentui-ui/toast@0.0.5. tsconfig.json configured with jsx: preserve and jsxImportSource: solid-js. Typecheck passes.
userStories.title: Install opentui, Solid.js, and opentui-ui packages

Proves the opentui + Bun stack works before any real panels are built. Full spec and Bun/opentui patterns: tasks/prd-watch-epic-1-foundation.md US-002.

Uses useKeyboard for q-key exit and useRenderer().destroy() for clean teardown. exitOnCtrlC: true handles Ctrl-C. `bun run src/cli.ts` (no args) launches the TUI; `bun run src/cli.ts --help` still shows help

userStories.notes: Added import for cmdWatch, case 'watch' dispatching to cmdWatch(args.slice(1)), default no-arg case calling cmdWatch([]), watch entry in help table as first command, and --help/-h/help handling. Typecheck passes.

acceptanceCriteria: src/watch/docker.ts extended with `export async function listChangedFiles(sessionId: string, showAll: boolean): Promise<DiffFile[]>`
DiffFile type exported: `{ status: 'M' | 'A' | 'D' | 'R'; path: string; oldPath?: string; mtime: number; noisy: boolean }`
Runs `docker exec <id> git -C /workspace diff --name-status yolomode-base` via Bun.spawn with piped I/O (never inherits raw TTY)
Parses M/A/D/R lines; renames (R100 etc.) use new path as `path` and old path as `oldPath`
Fetches mtime via `docker exec <id> stat -c %Y /workspace/<path>` — all in parallel with max 20 concurrent (use p-limit or manual batching)
Sorts by mtime descending; alphabetical path as tiebreaker
showAll=false: noisy files excluded entirely; showAll=true: included with noisy: true for UI dimming
Returns empty array (not error) when yolomode-base branch does not exist in container
Typecheck passes
userStories.description: Extend src/watch/docker.ts with `listChangedFiles(sessionId, showAll)`. Runs `git diff --name-status yolomode-base` via docker exec, parses M/A/D/R status, fetches mtime for each file in parallel (max 20 concurrent), sorts by mtime descending, filters noisy files via isNoisyFile(). Full spec: tasks/prd-watch-epic-4-diff-panel.md US-001.
userStories.notes: TDD: wrote 10 failing tests first (M/A/D parsing, R100/R095 renames with oldPath, mtime descending sort, alphabetical tiebreaker, noisy exclusion showAll=false, noisy inclusion showAll=true, empty output, sessionId passthrough). Extended src/watch/docker.ts with listChangedFiles() and DiffFile type. Uses injectable CommandRunner for test mocking. Manual batching for max 20 concurrent stat calls. All 87 bun tests pass, typecheck passes.
userStories.title: Implement listChangedFiles() in src/watch/docker.ts

acceptanceCriteria: TDD: write failing tests FIRST in src/watch/__tests__/logBuffer.test.ts — confirm they fail before implementing

Tests cover: newline-split chunk appended correctly, buffer under 5500 not trimmed, buffer over 5500 trimmed to 5000 (oldest dropped), empty chunk returns lines unchanged, chunk with no trailing newline handled
src/watch/utils/logBuffer.ts created with `export function manageBuffer(lines: string[], newChunk: string, cap: number): string[]`
Function is pure (no side effects, returns new array)
All bun tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.description: TDD: write failing bun tests first, then implement. Extract buffer management from the log streaming hook as pure function `manageBuffer(lines, newChunk, cap)`. Splits chunks into lines, appends, trims to 5000 when over 5500. Full spec: tasks/prd-watch-epic-3-agent-terminal.md US-001.
userStories.notes: TDD: wrote 11 failing tests first (newline-split, multi-line chunk, empty chunk no-op, no trailing newline, buffer under 5500 not trimmed, buffer over 5500 trimmed to cap with oldest dropped, immutability). Implemented manageBuffer() in src/watch/utils/logBuffer.ts as a pure function using hysteresis trim (trim at cap+500, slice to cap). All 11 bun tests pass, typecheck clean.
userStories.title: TDD: log buffer management pure function — manageBuffer (red-green)

acceptanceCriteria: src/watch/hooks/useLogStream.ts created
Accepts `sessionId: Accessor<string | null>` (reactive Solid accessor)
Returns `{ lines: Accessor<string[]>, clearLines: () => void }`
Uses `Bun.spawn(["docker", "logs", "--follow", "--tail", "200", id], { stdout: "pipe", stderr: "pipe" })`
Reads stdout with Web Streams API + TextDecoder loop; uses manageBuffer() from logBuffer.ts
Merges stderr into the same lines array
When sessionId() changes: kills previous process, clears lines, spawns new process
onCleanup cancels reader and kills the Bun.spawn process
5000-line buffer cap enforced via manageBuffer (trim triggered at 5500)
Typecheck passes
userStories.description: Create src/watch/hooks/useLogStream.ts as a Solid.js hook. Streams `docker logs --follow --tail 200` for the active session using Bun.spawn. Uses manageBuffer() from US-011. Restarts when sessionId changes. Adapt opendocker's packages/cli/src/components/panes/container/logs.tsx. Full spec: tasks/prd-watch-epic-3-agent-terminal.md US-001.
userStories.notes: TDD: wrote 8 tests first (null sessionId no-spawn, spawn with correct id, stdout accumulation, stderr accumulation, merged streams, clearLines reset, kill on dispose, empty initial lines). Created src/watch/hooks/useLogStream.ts with SpawnFn injectable interface. Uses createRenderEffect (createEffect is a no-op in Bun's server build of solid-js). Streams docker logs --follow --tail 200 via Bun.spawn, merges stdout+stderr via parallel readStream loops, caps buffer at 5000 lines via manageBuffer(), kills process on onCleanup. All 106 bun tests pass, typecheck passes.
userStories.title: Create useLogStream.ts — reactive docker log streaming hook

userStories.notes: Created src/watch/components/AgentTerminal.tsx using useLogStream(app.activeSession). Renders lines in <scrollbox> with stickyScroll autoscroll (stickyStart=bottom); s toggles pause with dim indicator; u/d scroll half terminal height; Home/End jump top/bottom. Wired as center column (flexGrow=1) in cmd-watch.tsx; right panel retains flexGrow=2. onCleanup for Bun.spawn handled by useLogStream internals. Typecheck passes, 106 bun tests pass.

userStories.description: TDD: write failing bun tests first, then implement. Pure function `haveFilesChanged(prev, next)` used by useDirtyPoller to detect when new files are changed in the container without triggering re-renders. Full spec: tasks/prd-watch-epic-4-diff-panel.md US-004.
userStories.notes: TDD: wrote 7 failing tests first (identical lists, added file, removed file, same files different order, empty→non-empty, non-empty→empty, both empty). Implemented pure haveFilesChanged() in src/watch/utils/fileListDiff.ts — sorts both arrays before comparing so order differences don't trigger false positives. 140 bun tests pass, typecheck clean.
userStories.title: TDD: file list change detection — haveFilesChanged (red-green)

acceptanceCriteria: src/watch/hooks/useDirtyPoller.ts created
Accepts `sessionId: Accessor<string | null>` and `rightPanelOpen: Accessor<boolean>`
Returns `{ dirty: Accessor<boolean>, clearDirty: () => void }`
Polls every 3000ms: `docker exec <id> git -C /workspace diff --name-only yolomode-base` via Bun.spawn (piped I/O)
Uses haveFilesChanged() from US-017; sets dirty to true when changed
Skips polling when rightPanelOpen() is false
Polling never triggers automatic panel re-renders (dirty is indicator only)
onCleanup clears the setInterval
Typecheck passes
userStories.description: Create src/watch/hooks/useDirtyPoller.ts as a Solid.js hook. Polls every 3s via docker exec, uses haveFilesChanged() from US-017, returns a dirty signal. Never auto-rerenders panels. Pauses when right panel is closed. Full spec: tasks/prd-watch-epic-4-diff-panel.md US-004.
userStories.notes: TDD: wrote 10 failing tests first (null sessionId no-exec, rightPanelOpen=false no-exec, sessionId passthrough, first poll establishes baseline without dirty, file change sets dirty=true, unchanged files keep dirty=false, clearDirty resets, clearDirty+stable list stays false, onCleanup clears interval). Implemented useDirtyPoller() in src/watch/hooks/useDirtyPoller.ts with injectable ExecFn interface. Uses setInterval checking sessionId() and rightPanelOpen() on each tick; null-baseline pattern skips dirty on first poll; haveFilesChanged() from US-017 drives detection. All 150 bun tests pass, typecheck clean.
userStories.title: Create useDirtyPoller.ts — 3s background poll for new changed files

] keypress toggles app.rightPanelOpen; panel uses `<Show when={app.rightPanelOpen()}>`
Panel width fixed at Math.floor(terminalWidth * 0.4) columns

r keypress calls refreshFileList() (re-fetches listChangedFiles) then clearDirty(); preserves selection if file still present, else selects first
useDirtyPoller wired into DiffPanel; passes app.rightPanelOpen so polling pauses when panel is closed

userStories.description: Create src/watch/components/DiffPanel.tsx containing FileList above DiffView with a horizontal divider. ] toggles the panel. Dirty indicator in header when useDirtyPoller fires. r refreshes file list and clears dirty. Full spec: tasks/prd-watch-epic-4-diff-panel.md US-005.
userStories.notes: Created src/watch/components/DiffPanel.tsx: stacks FileList above DiffView with a dim horizontal divider, wires useDirtyPoller to show [● new changes — r] in header, r refreshes file list with selection preservation (falls back to first file), f toggles full-diff mode (collapses FileList). Panel width = Math.floor(terminalWidth * 0.4). Extended FileList with refreshTrigger/onFilesLoaded props via createRenderEffect. Added ] global keybinding in cmd-watch.tsx to toggle rightPanelOpen; replaced placeholder with <Show when={app.rightPanelOpen()}><DiffPanel /></Show>. 150 bun tests pass, typecheck clean.
